text: The exercise involves grasping and picking up a blue ring from the right side of a ring tower. The participant navigates the ring around a wire structure without contact, using wrist articulation and supinated hand positioning.

The participant removes the blue ring from the wire structure using wrist articulation, passes it to the other hand, and navigates towards the left side of the screen to place the ring at the base of another wire structure.

The participant removes the ring from the top of the dome, holding it in a supinated position. To place the ring back around the wire structure, the participant approaches it in a pronated position, demonstrating wrist articulation and coordination.
The left hand grasps the second ring at the top of the dome in a supinated position, preparing to navigate the wire structure without contact.
The participant carefully maneuvers the ring around the wire structure, minimizing contact and force to prevent dislodging the loosely attached wire structure from the surface.

The operator navigates towards screen right, holding the ring in a pronated position to optimize range of motion. The ring is carefully placed back onto the wire structure, with slow and deliberate movements to avoid contact and minimize penalties.
